entries.definition:
l–ẓ–y fire, raging fire, to burn brightly; to be mad with anger.
Of this root, two words occur once each in the Qur’an: تَلَظَّى
talaẓẓā and لَظَى laẓā.

تَلَظَّى talaẓẓā [imperf., with elided initial ت of v. V intrans.] to
blaze, to burn fiercely (92:14) فَأَنذَرْتُكُمْ نَارًا تَلَظَّى so I have warned
you about a raging Fire.

لَظَى laẓā [n.] a raging fire (70:15) كَلَّا إِنَّهَا لَظَى indeed, it [Hell] is
truly a raging fire.
